name: Monkfish Kebabs
recipeIngredient: 1 Red or 1 green pepper (wash and deseed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the monkfish into 1-inch cubes.
2. Wash and deseed the pepper; cut it into 1-inch pieces.
3. To make the marinade, mix olive oil, lemon juice, crushed garlic clove, freeze-dried rosemary (if using), salt, and pepper in a glass or china bowl. Do not use metal.
4. Add the fish to the marinade and turn occasionally. Keep in the fridge for 90 minutes to 2 hours. It can stay in the marinade for up to six hours, but not longer.
5. When ready to cook, thread fish cubes onto skewers (no more than 3-4 cubes per skewer) with pepper pieces between each cube. Start and end with pepper.
6. Ensure all pieces touch but do not squash together. Push them to the sharp end, leaving a long handle for easy holding under the grill or barbecue.
7. Line the grill with foil (to save washing up) and grill for 5-6 minutes on each side. Do not place too close to the heat; they may burn outside before cooking inside.
8. The cubes should take about 10-12 minutes to cook properly, resulting in succulent meat with brown crispy bits on the outside.
9. Serve with a rice pilaf or crusty bread and lemon juice to squeeze over them.
